clues.clue: In this real-estate game, players buy properties like Boardwalk and Park Place.
In this game, players form words from lettered tiles on a 15-by-15 grid.
In this murder-mystery game, suspects include Colonel Mustard and Professor Plum.
This ancient two-player game of kings and pawns ends in checkmate.
In this grid game, you call out coordinates hoping to hear 'you sank my' fleet.
Chess is won by trapping the enemy king in this inescapable position.
In Monopoly, you win once every rival has gone this, out of money.
In Scrabble, playing all seven of your tiles at once earns this 50-point bonus.
Rolling five dice that all show the same number scores this game's namesake 50 points.
In Risk, victory means your armies occupy every one of these regions on the map.
Classic Monopoly tokens include a top hat, a thimble and this Scottie figure.
Chess sets include pawns, rooks, bishops, knights, a queen and this most important royal.
Connect Four players take turns dropping these colored plastic discs into a grid.
In Operation, you extract plastic ailments using these metal pincers.
In Sorry!, players slide these colored plastic playing pieces around the track.
Rolling these two pip-covered cubes determines movement in many games.
In Monopoly, landing on the wrong corner can send you here, behind bars.
In Trivial Pursuit, correct answers earn these colored plastic 'pie' wedges.
In backgammon, you may bear pieces off only after moving them all into this final quadrant.
In Scrabble, a tile placed on a dark-red premium square multiplies the word by this factor.
In this island game, players gather brick, wood, sheep, wheat and ore to build settlements.
In this cooperative game, players race to cure four diseases sweeping the globe.
In this game, players collect colored cards to claim cross-country rail routes.
In this tile-laying game, you place 'meeples' on a growing medieval French landscape.
In this hidden-role party game, villagers try to unmask the secret lycanthropes among them.

final.clue: Add up the pips on every face of one standard six-sided die and you get this total.
